692662bc653007f6f742166a.question: A bridge in this city was retrofitted with a spherical wheelchair lift to alleviate concerns of inaccessibility, which was later scrapped on account of being too slow and overheating. That structure in this city, the Constitution Bridge, was designed by Santiago Calatrava and criticized for being out of place with this city’s architecture. An important public transportation network in this city utilizes vehicles called by a term meaning “little (*) steamers”. Alex Hai, who received recognition for supposedly being the first woman in a certain transit occupation in this city, came out as a trans man in 2017. A straw hat and blue- or red-and-white striped top make up the uniforms of certain transit operators in this city. “Vaporetti” are the name of the water buses in, for 10 points, what Italian city whose canals are home to many gondoliers?
692662bc653007f6f742166a.question_sanitized: A bridge in this city was retrofitted with a spherical wheelchair lift to alleviate concerns of inaccessibility, which was later scrapped on account of being too slow and overheating. "Vaporetti" are the name of the water buses in, for 10 points, what Italian city whose canals are home to many gondoliers?

692662bd653007f6f7421698.question: A ferry terminal on this river is featured on the cover of Martin Scorsese's Bob Dylan biopic No Direction Home. Thomas Penson the younger designed a number of bridges over this river, including the Caerhowel Bridge. The Hampton Loade Ferry, a pedestrian cable ferry, has crossed this river for over 400 years. The world's first major bridge to be made of iron was constructed in 1781 across this river. Sir John Hawkshaw designed a railway tunnel under this river which was completed in 1886 and was the longest underwater railway tunnel in the world until 1987. A 1966 bridge across this river was redesignated as the (*) M48 after the 1996 completion of a new, more direct bridge to carry the M4 between Bristol and Newport. For ten points, identify this river which forms part of the southern border between England and Wales.

692662bd653007f6f74216a6.question: The wreck of this man’s ship, the Contamana, would later be moved to a city he established, Puerto Maldonado. This man discovered a portage that linked the Serjhali and Caspajhali rivers, which flowed into the Urubamba and Madre de Dios rivers respectively, and demonstrated its suitability for transportation with a publicity stunt. That publicity stunt was dramatized in a film whose contentious production was chronicled in the documentary Burden of Dreams, in which this man is played by Klaus Kinski. An (*) “isthmus” in the Peruvian jungle is named after, for 10 points, what rubber magnate who had his steamship disassembled and carried across it, inspiring a 1982 film by Werner Herzog in which a ship is dragged whole across a mountain?
692662bd653007f6f74216a6.question_sanitized: The wreck of this man's ship, the Contamana, would later be moved to a city he established, Puerto Maldonado. This man discovered a portage that linked the Serjhali and Caspajhali rivers, which flowed into the Urubamba and Madre de Dios rivers respectively, and demonstrated its suitability for transportation with a publicity stunt. That publicity stunt was dramatized in a film whose contentious production was chronicled in the documentary Burden of Dreams, in which this man is played by Klaus Kinski. An (*) "isthmus" in the Peruvian jungle is named after, for 10 points, what rubber magnate who had his steamship disassembled and carried across it, inspiring a 1982 film by Werner Herzog in which a ship is dragged whole across a mountain?
692662bd653007f6f74216a6.answer: Carlos Fermin Fitzcarrald [accept Fitzcarraldo; prompt on Bryan Sweeney Fitzgerald by asking “what was the name of the real-life person who inspired that character?”]

692662bd653007f6f74216af.question: The construction of an infrastructure project employing this technology has been delayed by government concern over the Ōi River leaking into its tunnels. A descendant of HSST, a form of this technology developed in Japan, is currently in use on the Linimo line in Nagoya. This technology will be used by the planned Chūō Shinkansen, which has already set (*) speed records on its test track at Yamanashi. A train line that uses this technology is the fastest operating electric train in the world, reaching a speed of 268 mph on its 30km trip between Pudong Airport and Longyang Road station in downtown Shanghai. For 10 points, identify this technology that propels trains by suspending them above the ground and pulling them with a series of electromagnets.
